methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: User calls Genesis.participate(pointAmt, virtualsAmt)
Balance and allowance checks on virtualTokenAddress
mapAddrToVirtuals[msg.sender] += virtualsAmt, participants.push if first time
safeTransferFrom(msg.sender, address(this), virtualsAmt)
data_flows.trust_changes: User funds move to contract custody; off-chain system controls pointAmt interpretation
exposure_risks: pointAmt is only emitted, not stored — off-chain system fully controls points allocation
manipulation_risks: No per-user cumulative cap — a single user can call participate() many times bypassing maxContributionVirtualAmount
Off-chain point system can be manipulated since on-chain has no point validation

path: FGenesis.onGenesisSuccess calls Genesis.onGenesisSuccess with operator-supplied arrays
Validates refund amounts against mapAddrToVirtuals
If first launch: approves agentFactory, calls initFromBondingCurve + executeBondingCurveApplication
Refunds VIRTUAL tokens to specified addresses
Sets claimableAgentTokens mapping for distribution addresses
transformations: VIRTUAL tokens → AgentFactory → Agent Token + DAO + NFT
data_flows.trust_changes: FACTORY_ROLE fully controls refund and distribution amounts — no on-chain validation of fairness
exposure_risks: claimableAgentTokens uses assignment (=) not addition (+=) — repeated calls overwrite unclaimed allocations
Admin can call withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized to drain unclaimed agent tokens
manipulation_risks: Operator can distribute agent tokens unfairly since distribution amounts are not validated against contributions
Multiple calls overwrite prior claimableAgentTokens entries, stealing from users who haven't claimed
No on-chain check that total distributed agent tokens proportionally match virtual contributions

data_flows.source: Any user via launchFor() (launch() is broken)
path: User calls launchFor with purchaseAmount
Fee deducted and sent to _feeTo
Remaining transferred to Bonding contract
FERC20 token created with initialSupply
Pair created via factory, initial liquidity added to pair
Initial purchase executed via router.buy
FERC20 tokens transferred to msg.sender
transformations: FERC20 minted with full supply to Bonding
Initial buy swaps asset for FERC20
data_flows.trust_changes: creator parameter is user-controlled in launchFor — any address can be attributed as creator
exposure_risks: launchFor() is public — anyone can attribute any address as token creator affecting downstream rewards
launch() function is broken due to double nonReentrant modifier — always reverts
manipulation_risks: Creator attribution manipulation — attacker sets victim as creator, or sets themselves as creator of popular token
Since launch() always fails, only launchFor() works, and caller != creator is possible

data_flows.source: Triggered automatically during buy() when reserveA <= gradThreshold
path: buy() detects graduation threshold reached
_openTradingOnUniswap called
Bonding curve trading disabled
Asset tokens transferred from pair to Bonding
Bonding approves agentFactory for asset balance
initFromBondingCurve + executeBondingCurveApplication called
Agent token created, pair approved for agent token transfers
FERC20 tokens burned from pair (but totalSupply NOT decremented)
transformations: FERC20 pair liquidity → Agent token creation via AgentFactory
sinks: FERC20 balances zeroed but totalSupply unchanged
data_flows.trust_changes: Automated — no human in the loop for graduation decision
exposure_risks: FERC20.burnFrom does NOT decrement totalSupply — phantom supply remains
Stored _token.data.supply used in executeBondingCurveApplication may be stale
manipulation_risks: Graduation can be front-run — MEV bots can sandwich the graduation transaction
Exact gradThreshold boundary can be gamed — buy exactly enough to trigger

path: EXECUTOR records txhashes and amounts
Accumulated tax tracked in agentTaxAmounts
_swapForAsset swaps taxToken to assetToken via router
creatorFee (99% default) sent to creator address
feeAmount (1% default) sent to treasury
Optional tbaBonus.distributeBonus called
data_flows.trust_changes: EXECUTOR_ROLE provides minOutput — could set to 0 enabling sandwich attacks
exposure_risks: MEV/sandwich attacks if minOutput is set too low by executor
Cached TBA/creator in _agentRecipients can become stale if NFT ownership changes
manipulation_risks: Creator address can be changed by current creator via updateCreator — tax revenue can be redirected
dcaSell uses input-amount-based slippage calculation which is mathematically incorrect

path: Caller provides srcTokenAddress and list of accounts
For each account: check FERC20 balance
Burn FERC20 from account (owner-only burnFrom, Bonding is owner)
TransferFrom agent tokens from pairAddress to account
sinks: Agent tokens transferred to user accounts
data_flows.trust_changes: No access control — anyone can force-unwrap any user's tokens
exposure_risks: Users have no choice in unwrapping — anyone can force-convert their FERC20 to agent tokens
burnFrom doesn't reduce totalSupply — accounting mismatch
manipulation_risks: If agent token has different value/decimals than assumed, 1:1 exchange rate is incorrect

path: _castVote records vote
Score incremented for voter
If support==1 (For) and params provided, _updateMaturity called for ELO scoring
If support==1, _tryAutoExecute checks if forVotes == totalSupply
If condition met, execute(proposalId) called inline
data_flows.trust_changes: Voting power snapshot taken at proposal creation — no flash loan protection beyond that
exposure_risks: getMaturity divides by forVotes — division by zero if forVotes is 0
Auto-execute happens during castVote — complex execution during vote can have unexpected side effects
manipulation_risks: If totalSupply is 0 or very small, a single voter can auto-execute any proposal
ELO params (uint8[] votes) are voter-provided and could be crafted to game maturity scores

data_flows.source: Caller provides token, recipients, amounts, total
path: Assembly transferFrom pulls _total from caller to Airdrop contract
Loop transfers amounts[i] to recipients[i]
data_flows.trust_changes: No validation on array lengths or sum(amounts) vs _total
exposure_risks: No check that _recipients.length == _amounts.length — mismatched arrays read garbage calldata
No check that sum(amounts) == _total — excess tokens stuck in contract permanently
manipulation_risks: If _recipients shorter than _amounts, tokens sent to garbage addresses derived from calldata

core_rules: Genesis lifecycle: Not Started → Active → Ended → (Success | Failed | Cancelled)
Bonding curve: constant product k=xy with virtual liquidity (no real asset tokens in pair initially)
Graduation: automatic when token reserve <= gradThreshold during buy
Tax distribution: taxToken swapped to assetToken, split between creator and treasury
DAO proposals can be auto-executed when 100% for-votes (unanimous)
FERC20 tokens are intermediary — unwrapped to agent tokens post-graduation
VirtualGenesisDAO supports early execution when quorum + vote success conditions met
economic_incentives: Bonding curve fee: split between buy/sell taxes sent to taxVault
AgentTax: 99% to creator, 1% to treasury (default feeRate=100/10000)
TBABonus: additional 35% bonus from separate allowance pool
Genesis participation: contribute VIRTUAL tokens for agent token allocation (determined off-chain)
Validator scoring: ELO-based system rewards accurate model evaluation
governance_mechanisms: AgentDAO: quorum fraction 51%, auto-execute on 100% for-votes
VirtualGenesisDAO: configurable quorum (default 10000e18), early execution via EXECUTOR_ROLE
ContributionNft: proposal-proposer-only minting, admin bypass for proposal threshold

edge_cases.scenario: Bonding.launch() always reverts due to double nonReentrant modifier
edge_cases.expected_behavior: launch() should create token and return pair address
edge_cases.actual_behavior: launch() calls launchFor() which also has nonReentrant — internal call re-enters guard and reverts
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: launch() is permanently broken; only launchFor() works, which allows any caller to set arbitrary creator address, affecting who receives graduation rewards and AgentTax creator fees

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Burning tokens should reduce totalSupply to maintain supply conservation invariant
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only _balances[user] is decremented; _totalSupply remains at initial value creating phantom supply
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: After graduation, FERC20 totalSupply is inflated. Any code relying on totalSupply (maxTx calculation, external integrations) uses wrong values. Supply passed to executeBondingCurveApplication uses stored data.supply which may diverge from actual circulating supply

edge_cases.scenario: Genesis.onGenesisSuccess called multiple times overwrites claimableAgentTokens
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Agent token allocations should be final and immutable once set
edge_cases.actual_behavior: claimableAgentTokens uses = assignment not +=. Second call overwrites unclaimed allocations
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Malicious or compromised OPERATION_ROLE can call onGenesisSuccess again with different distribution amounts, reducing or zeroing out unclaimed user allocations

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only truly leftover assets should be withdrawable
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Admin can withdraw ANY token including agent tokens that users haven't claimed yet
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Admin calls withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized for agentTokenAddress, stealing tokens that users are entitled to claim via claimAgentToken

edge_cases.expected_behavior: Slippage should be calculated based on expected output from AMM
edge_cases.actual_behavior: minOutput = (amountToSwap * (DENOM - slippage)) / DENOM — uses input amount as basis, not expected output
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If taxToken and assetToken have very different values, minOutput is either way too high (always reverting) or way too low (no slippage protection, enabling sandwich attacks)

edge_cases.scenario: AgentDAO.getMaturity division by zero when forVotes is 0
edge_cases.actual_behavior: _proposalMaturities[proposalId] / forVotes reverts with division by zero
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Calling getMaturity on a proposal with zero for-votes causes revert, potentially breaking integrations that call this view function

edge_cases.scenario: Airdrop contract has no array length validation
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Uses _amounts.length as loop bound; reads _recipients from raw calldata offset — mismatched arrays read garbage data
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If arrays mismatch, tokens sent to unintended addresses derived from adjacent calldata. Also sum(amounts) vs _total not validated — excess tokens permanently stuck in contract

edge_cases.scenario: ContributionNft.mint doesn't verify proposal is accepted
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only accepted/succeeded proposals should have contribution NFTs minted
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only checks msg.sender == proposalProposer — no state check on proposal
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Proposer can mint ContributionNft before proposal voting completes or even for rejected proposals, polluting the contribution history

edge_cases.scenario: Genesis.participate has no per-user cumulative contribution cap
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Users should have reasonable contribution limits
edge_cases.actual_behavior: maxContributionVirtualAmount is per-transaction, not per-user. Users can call participate() unlimited times
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Whale can dominate genesis participation by making many transactions, each under the per-tx limit

edge_cases.scenario: Bonding.sell() and buy() lack nonReentrant modifier
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Functions modifying state after external calls should have reentrancy protection
edge_cases.actual_behavior: tokenInfo data updates happen after router calls which involve external token transfers
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If traded token has callbacks (ERC777-like), attacker could re-enter sell/buy to manipulate tokenInfo state before it's updated

edge_cases.scenario: FPair.swap does not verify k-constant invariant
edge_cases.expected_behavior: After swap, newReserve0 * newReserve1 should equal k
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Reserves updated blindly based on router-provided amounts; k stored but never verified
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Router bugs could violate constant product invariant without detection, leading to economic exploitation of the pair

edge_cases.scenario: Bonding price calculation integer division precision loss
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Price should be calculated with adequate precision
edge_cases.actual_behavior: price = newReserveA / newReserveB — pure integer division loses all fractional precision
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Price tracking is inaccurate, though only used for informational purposes in tokenInfo storage

edge_cases.scenario: AgentInference.promptMulti first element agentId could match uninitialized prevAgentId
edge_cases.expected_behavior: TBA should always be looked up correctly
edge_cases.actual_behavior: prevAgentId initialized to 0; if first agentIds[0] is 0, TBA lookup is skipped, agentTba stays address(0)
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Edge case with agentId 0 would cause safeTransferFrom to address(0) to revert, but agentId 0 is unlikely valid in the system

assumption_violations: FERC20 assumes burnFrom correctly updates supply — it does NOT, violating ERC20 supply conservation
Bonding assumes launch() wrapper works — it DOES NOT due to double nonReentrant, forcing direct launchFor usage
Genesis assumes onGenesisSuccess is called once per genesis — nothing prevents multiple calls after first launch
AgentTax dcaSell assumes input and output tokens have comparable value for slippage calculation — this is generally false
FPair assumes router always provides correct swap amounts maintaining k — no on-chain verification
ContributionNft.ownerOf returns address(0) for non-existent tokens instead of reverting, violating ERC721 spec
FGenesis.createGenesis uses raw transferFrom instead of SafeERC20, assuming VIRTUAL token returns bool
game_theory_risks: Genesis participation: whale can dominate allocation by splitting contributions across many transactions under maxContributionVirtualAmount
Bonding curve graduation can be front-run: MEV bot buys just before graduation to profit from price discontinuity
AgentTax creator update: current creator can transfer creator rights to another address, redirecting all future tax revenue
DAO auto-execution on 100% for-votes creates race condition: last voter triggers execution, potentially with different gas conditions
launchFor() creator attribution: anyone can launch a token attributing it to a popular address, potentially confusing users or gaming creator rewards
Validator ELO scoring: voters provide their own battle results (uint8[] votes param) — if not validated off-chain, scores can be gamed
integration_risks: Genesis → AgentFactoryV3: initFromBondingCurve and executeBondingCurveApplication are called sequentially — if factory state changes between calls, inconsistency possible
Bonding → AgentFactoryV3: graduation path depends on factory granting BONDING_ROLE and factory's executeBondingCurveApplication working correctly
AgentTax → IRouter: swap relies on external AMM — price manipulation or low liquidity causes failed or exploitable swaps
AeroAdaptor: hardcoded route (stable=false) — if pool params change, swaps may fail or route through suboptimal path
FGenesis grants BONDING_ROLE on AgentFactoryV3 to each new Genesis contract — requires FGenesis to have role admin on AgentFactoryV3
AgentDAO → ServiceNft.mint: triggered via proposal execution — relies on contribution and service NFT contracts being correctly configured
calculation_errors: Bonding.sell/buy: price = reserveA / reserveB uses integer division losing all precision for values < 1
Bonding liquidity calculation: liquidity = (((K * 10000) / assetRate) * 10000 ether / supply) * 1 ether / 10000 — complex multiplication/division chain susceptible to precision loss and overflow
AgentTax dcaSell: minOutput = amountToSwap * (DENOM - slippage) / DENOM — calculates slippage on input amount instead of expected output amount
AgentDAO.getMaturity: _proposalMaturities[proposalId] / forVotes — division by zero when forVotes is 0
FRouter buy tax: txFee = (fee * amountIn) / 100 — fee is from factory (e.g., 1 for 1%), but if fee > 100, tax exceeds 100%

token_semantics.notes: ERC20Capped with owner-only mint. Standard transfer. Used as base currency throughout protocol.
CRITICAL: burnFrom/burn do NOT decrement totalSupply. Has maxTx limit on non-excluded addresses. Owner can burn anyone's tokens without approval. Intermediary bonding curve token.
Non-transferable voting token. approve/transfer/transferFrom all revert. Only owner can update balances via oracleTransfer. ERC20Votes for governance snapshots.
Created via AgentFactoryV3.executeBondingCurveApplication. Exact implementation not provided but assumed standard ERC20. Used as final agent token post-graduation.

invariants.name: ferc20_supply_conservation_BROKEN
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: _totalSupply is never decremented by burn/burnFrom. After any burn, sum(_balances) < _totalSupply.

invariants.name: genesis_virtual_conservation
invariants.notes: Should hold that contract balance >= sum of all unreturned mapAddrToVirtuals entries. Maintained by participate/refund/fail logic.

invariants.name: fpair_constant_product
invariants.notes: k set at mint time. swap() does NOT verify reserve0*reserve1 == k after update. Router is trusted to provide correct amounts.

invariants.name: agent_tax_swap_tracking
invariants.notes: amountSwapped should never exceed amountCollected. The difference represents pending swap amount.

invariants.name: genesis_claimable_token_integrity
invariants.notes: VULNERABLE: onGenesisSuccess can be called multiple times overwriting allocations. withdrawLeftAssetsAfterFinalized can drain tokens backing claims.

invariants.name: tba_bonus_allowance
invariants.notes: _agentPaidAmounts should never exceed _agentAllowances. Enforced by setAllowances check and bonus capping logic.
